page.title: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome) — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Polycystic Ovary Syndrome: 116 alterations and 177 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Curcumin may have the potential to enhance cardiometabolic health by reducing hyperglycemia, insulin resistance, and serum TC levels in women with PCOS.
Green tea promotes weight loss in women with polycystic ovary syndrome.
Impact of ketogenic diet on weight, metabolic, and endocrine parameters in women with polycystic ovary syndrome.
Ketogenic diet was associated with lower weight loss parameters, including weight, BMI, WC, and FM, in obese or overweight women with PCOS.
The effects of ketogenic diet on metabolic and hormonal parameters in patients with polycystic ovary syndrome.
The effects of N-acetylcysteine supplement on metabolic parameters in women with polycystic ovary syndrome.
This meta-analysis suggested that quercetin has positive effects on PCOS treatment.
Vitamin E can positively affect metabolic and hormonal parameters in women with PCOS.
This meta-analysis suggests that consumption of tea supplementation in women with PCOS could significantly decrease the levels of FBG and FINS.
Berberine showed a promising prospect in treating polycystic ovary syndrome patients with insulin resistance.
BPA might be involved in the insulin-resistance and hyperandrogenism of polycystic ovary syndrome.
Cinnamon can be used as an anti-diabetic agent and an add-on treatment to control glycemic indices among patients with T2D or PCOS.
N-acetyl-cysteine may be considered as an alternative supplement to metformin in PCOS patients.
Serum 25-hydroxyvitamin D, follicle stimulating hormone and sex hormone-binding globulin were significantly lower in PCOS patients than controls.
Serum level of vitamin D was associated with the risk of polycystic ovary syndrome.
Vitamin D improves levels of hormonal, oxidative stress and inflammatory parameters in polycystic ovary syndrome.
Vitamin D supplementation had beneficial on biomarkers of inflammation and oxidative stress among women with polycystic ovary syndrome.
Vitamin D supplementation improves markers of polycystic ovarian syndrome.
Vitamin D supplementation is sufficient for decreasing serum triglyceride.
Electroacupuncture to improve endometrial receptivity and folliculogenesis in polycystic ovary syndrome.
"Hyperandrogenism in women with polycystic ovary syndrome persists after menopause."
12-week L-carnitine supplementation in overweight or obese women with PCOS ameliorate insulin resistance.
A ketogenic diet followed by gradual carbohydrate reintroduction restores menstrual cycles in women with polycystic ovary syndrome.
A ketogenic diet intervention is beneficial for overweight/obese women with PCOS with different serum uric acid concentrations.
A single bout of electroacupuncture remodels epigenetic and transcriptional changes in adipose tissue in polycystic ovary syndrome.
Abdominal acupuncture treatment can improve the endocrine and metabolic function of patients with obesity-type polycystic ovarian syndrome.
Acetyl-L-carnitine ameliorates metabolic and endocrine alterations in women with PCOS.
Acupuncture at Zhibian through Shuidao could significantly improve the basic temperature of patients with polycystic ovary syndrome, and regulate hormone level of reproductive endocrine.
Acupuncture can effectively reduce FINS, HOMA-IR and WC levels in PCOS patients with abdominal obesity.
Acupuncture improves the emotion domain and lipid profiles in women with polycystic ovarian syndrome.
Administration of B-group vitamins reduces circulating homocysteine in polycystic ovarian syndrome patients treated with metformin.
Adrenal androgen excess in the polycystic ovary syndrome patients is related to an exaggerated secretory response of the adrenal cortex for DHEA and androstenedione (A4).
Alpha lipoic acid administration improved both peripheral sensitivity to insulin and liver clearance of insulin reducing potential risk of diabetes and NAFLD.
An 8-week low-starch/low-dairy diet resulted in weight loss, improved insulin sensitivity and reduced testosterone in women with PCOS.
Astaxanthin supplementation impact on insulin resistance, lipid profile, blood pressure, and oxidative stress in polycystic ovary syndrome patients.
Astaxanthin treatment ameliorates ER stress in polycystic ovary syndrome patients.
Astaxanthin treatment decreases pro-inflammatory cytokines and improves reproductive outcomes in patients with polycystic ovary syndrome undergoing assisted reproductive technology.
Astaxanthin treatment has been shown to increase both serum TAC levels and activation of the Nrf2 axis in PCOS patients' GCs.
Beneficial effects of a novel fenugreek seed extract in women with polycystic ovary syndrome.
Beneficial effects of ketogenic diet in women with polycystic ovary syndrome and liver dysfunction who are obese.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: PCOS is a lifelong endocrine disorder; complete spontaneous resolution rare; some phenotypic improvement with menopause; fertility returns in 50-70% with ovulation induction; metabolic features persist
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Weight loss of 5-10% restores ovulation in 55-90% of anovulatory PCOS women; metformin restores ovulation in 30-50%; letrozole (ovulation induction): 27% live birth rate per cycle (PPCOS II); lifestyle modification: 50-80% improvement in menstrual regularity
remission.gap_size: Large — lifestyle modification and metformin are evidence-based first-line but most patients receive OCPs (treating symptoms only, not the underlying metabolic disorder)
remission.barrier_detail: OCP (oral contraceptive pill) is most commonly prescribed for PCOS, treating symptoms (irregular periods, hirsutism) without addressing the underlying insulin resistance and metabolic risk; OCP does not treat infertility and may worsen insulin sensitivity; the PCOS field has lacked a clear pharmacotherapy standard
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — International PCOS Guideline updated; letrozole formally recommended over clomiphene for ovulation induction; metformin recommended as first-line metabolic treatment; GLP-1 agonists emerging as adjunct; spironolactone for hyperandrogenism; first guidelines were 2011 (Endocrine Society)
remission.outdated_treatments: Clomiphene citrate as first-line ovulation induction (inferior to letrozole — NEJM 2014 head-to-head); OCP as sole long-term treatment without metabolic assessment; ovarian drilling (laparoscopic — rarely indicated with letrozole available)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Inositol supplementation (myo-inositol + D-chiro-inositol) — multiple RCTs showing improvement in menstrual regularity, ovulation, insulin sensitivity comparable to metformin in some studies; dietary interventions (low-glycaemic diet — reduces insulin, androgens); N-acetylcysteine (NAC) as alternative to metformin in some cohorts; berberine
remission.frontier_signal: GLP-1 agonists (semaglutide significantly improves ovarian function, menstrual regularity, and fertility outcomes in PCOS — SPGP trial Phase 3); tirzepatide for PCOS metabolic phenotype; progesterone receptor modulators; gut microbiome modulation
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
